mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE momentum and 20-day forward returns exceeds 0.05 in absolute value, or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines by >0.2pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal robust consumer demand and economic expansion, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved profit growth visibility.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month growth rate and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When federal funds rate exceeds 1.5%, monetary tightening cycle reduces liquidity available for equity risk-taking. This triggers a negative signal for S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors rotate away from equities in anticipation of slower growth.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above 1.5% annualized rate, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This interaction with stable inflation produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors recognize sustainable profit expansion without margin compression.
failure_condition: When INDPRO growth > 1.5% annualized and CPIAUCSL stable, positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline by more than 10% over 3 months, signaling weakening demand for residential investment and forward-looking economic contraction. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 560.0 thousand units as of 2016-11
failure_condition: When HOUST declines > 10% over 3 months, negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100k per month while unemployment remains elevated above 8%, signaling labor market slack and weak demand. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors recognize insufficient momentum for earnings growth.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS < 100k and UNRATE > 8%, negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances, or Rank IC of interaction < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth accelerates above 2.5% annualized while retail sales momentum remains positive, signaling broad-based economic expansion. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors increase equity allocations in response to sustained growth visibility.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized and RSAFS growth > 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 > 2.5% and RSAFS growth > 0.3%, positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances, or Rank IC of interaction < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 2.5% annualized while federal funds rate remains below 1%, signaling monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This regime produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors seek equity hedges against inflation erosion of cash holdings.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL growth > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1%, positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances, or Rank IC of interaction < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.
